stem: A chemistry teacher is preparing the 8 stations of a chemistry laboratory. Each station will have either Experiment X materials or Experiment Y materials, but not both. Experiment X requires 5 grams of salt, and Experiment Y requires 3 grams of salt. If x is the number of stations that will be set up for Experiment X and the remaining stations will be set up for Experiment Y, which of the following equations indicates that a total of 40 grams of salt is needed for all 8 stations?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The number of Experiment Y stations is 8 − x. Total salt = 5x (from Experiment X stations) + 3(8 − x) (from Experiment Y stations) = 40.

Choices A, B, and C fail to correctly account for the 8 − x Experiment Y stations.
